senses.definition: to let something happen or exist

examples.x: The large venue can admit of a substantial audience for the concert.
This plan admits of various approaches to problem-solving.
The design of the software should admit of future updates and improvements.
The contract should admit of renegotiation if necessary.

senses.definition: to ask about what has happened or will happen to someone or something

examples.x: I dread to think what will become of them if they lose their home.
What became of that student who used to live with you?
What became of those Chinese vases that Mum used to have?
What would become of her now?
Whatever will become of Sam when his wife dies?

senses.definition: to be formed from particular parts or things

examples.x: The salad consists of fresh greens, tomatoes, and a zesty vinaigrette.
The committee consists of representatives from various departments.
The novel consists of three main plotlines that intertwine throughout the narrative.
The team consists of experienced professionals with diverse skill sets.
The success of the recipe largely consists of the unique combination of spices used.

senses.definition: to take away or deny someone or something the possession or enjoyment of a particular thing
to selflessly contribute one's time, energy, etc. to a task or cause, typically for the benefit of others or a greater purpose

examples.x: She's always willing to give of her time to volunteer at the local shelter.
He was always willing to give of his time to help his elderly neighbors with their errands.
They decided to give of their savings to support a humanitarian mission.
The teacher is admired for giving of her energy to create an engaging and inspiring learning environment.

senses.definition: to know about somebody or something because one has received information or news about them

examples.x: Have you heard of the recent company merger?
This is the first I've heard of your plans to travel the world; it sounds amazing.
The last I heard of him, he had moved to New York and started a new job.
I was shocked to hear of their breakup; they seemed so happy together.
I never heard of such a thing.

senses.definition: to be aware of someone or something and have some information about them, although the knowledge may be limited

examples.x: I know of a good restaurant in the city, but I haven't been there myself.
They were talking about popular books and knew of a bestselling author.
I know of a local bakery that makes delicious pastries.
She knows of a good movie that was released recently.
They know of a reliable mechanic who can fix their car.

senses.definition: to indicate, foretell, or suggest something

examples.x: The old, creaking floorboards spoke of the age and history of the abandoned mansion.
The significant drop in stock prices speaks of uncertainty in the financial markets.
The subtle smile on her face spoke of a hidden joy that she didn't openly express.

senses.definition: to acknowledge a specific concept, suggestion, or factor

examples.x: Always think of your health when making lifestyle choices.
It's essential to think of the consequences before making a significant decision.
Before making any investment, think of your long-term financial goals.
As a leader, always think of the well-being of your team.
I didn’t think of the impact on others before making that choice.

senses.definition: to be a cause or source of division or conflict between two or more parties

examples.x: Religious differences can come between people of different faiths.
Political ideologies often come between individuals with opposing views.
Cultural misunderstandings can come between people from different backgrounds.
Economic disparities can come between different social classes in society.
Language barriers can come between individuals trying to communicate effectively.

senses.definition: to be in a confined area with little space between two objects or individuals

examples.x: I felt sandwiched between two large suitcases in the crowded bus.
She was sandwiched between two colleagues in the packed elevator.
The car was so narrow that I felt sandwiched between the driver and the door.
The narrow alleyway had us sandwiched between high brick walls, and there was no escape.
In the crowded subway, I found myself sandwiched between two commuters.
